02
Source VMS Utilization — CPS Savings of ~0.1 USD across CN Lanes

Lever 1 — Mandatory pgroup / Master Carton Utilization

Target Fill Rate
115% of Box Size
Mandatory minimum fill enforced at packing station before pgroup is sealed and dispatched.
MetricCN-SACN-AE
Avg Utilization (Current)135%120%
P10 Utilization (Current)100%97%
Target Utilization≥ 115%
Project Savings (USD)5,0406,912
Projected Savings (CPS)0.080.15

Lever 2 — AI Validation of Pre-Seal Photo

1
Packer takes photo of filled carton before sealing
2
AI model validates fill level against 115% standard
✓
Pass → Pgroup sealed & dispatched
✗
Fail → Alert raised, packer refills before proceeding
Fewer cartons
Lower freight cost
Better cube utilization
Max billable weight
Sample AI Extraction Outputs
AI model detects individual items within cartons and computes box utilization score. Yellow bounding boxes indicate detected packages. Scores below 115% trigger a refill alert.
03
MPS Holding for ZATCA Savings (KSA) — ZATCA savings of ~1.8 USD per shipment

Shipments held in structured time windows to maximize consolidation. Non-consolidated shipments trigger ZATCA cross-charge to seller.

Fast Track
48h
High-velocity SKUs · Frequent departures
Standard
72h
Standard velocity · Default holding window
Extended
96h
Cost Conscious Velocity · Max consolidation window
Outcome Paths

✓ Consolidated

Shipment linked to MPS master or another secondary within the holding window. No ZATCA penalty — normal processing.

✗ Not Consolidated

Shipment exits as single or another MPS after holding window expires.

Seller Penalty Structure Incremental ZATCA recovered from sellers at fault
Holding Bucket CN-SA Lane
Additional ZATCA (CPS, USD) % Coverage of MPS Ship to FA Increase
48 hours1.257%2 days
72 hours1.572%3 days
96 hours1.881%4 days
* 96h is the current holding period. The 1.8 USD savings is against December when we paid complete ZATCA against every shipment.
04
China Local Vendor — Linehaul Rate Changes ~0.1–0.3 USD/shipment cost benefit

Introducing local Chinese vendors as linehaul partners creates competitive rate pressure and drives savings on key China → GCC corridors.

Before

Single Vendor Model
  • No competitive pressure on rates
  • Limited negotiating leverage
  • Rate increases absorbed by Noon
Rate: 7–8 USD/kg (Incumbent)
→

After

Multi-Vendor Competition
  • Local vendors introduced as linehaul partners
  • Rate benchmarking + competitive bids
  • Savings passed through on key lanes
Rate: 5–7 USD/kg (↓ Local Vendor)
Rate Card — Key Lanes
Lane Incumbent Rate (USD/kg) Local Vendor Rate (USD/kg) CPS Reduction (USD) Remarks
China → UAE (Prime to EPIC)7.655.80.28Executed in June
China → UAE (SF to EPIC)6.05.90.09TAT degradation of 1 day
